About: It is about an invasion of Western Europe by the Warsaw Pact.
It is about a surprise attack by Warsaw Pact forces against NATO forces defending Western Europe in the 1970s.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It is associated with Decision Games.
It is associated with Compass Games.
It is associated with Strategy & Tactics #220.
It is associated with The Comprehensive Guide to Board Wargaming.
It is associated with Phoenix (#14).
It is associated with The Wargamer (Issue 31).
It is associated with Yaquinto Games.
It is associated with The Guide to Simulations/Games for Education and Training.
It is associated with the 2005 Origins Awards.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a hex grid map of Central Europe.
It consists of 3 pages of rules errata.
HasA: It has four scenarios.
It has Victory Points.
It has a hex grid map of Central Europe.
It has a north-south axis of Denmark to Switzerland.
It has an east-west axis of Poland to Belgium.
It has a scale of 15 miles per hex.
It has a 36-page rulebook.
It has 3 pages of rules errata.
It has die-cut counters.
It has tactical nuclear weapons.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a surprise attack by Warsaw Pact forces against NATO forces defending Western Europe in the 1970s.
It is characterized by a hex grid map of Central Europe with a north-south axis of Denmark to Switzerland and an east-west axis of Poland to Belgium.
It is characterized by a scale of 15 miles per hex.
It is characterized by four scenarios.
It is characterized by Victory Points earned by destroying enemy units and capturing or recapturing cities.
It is characterized by scenarios lasting 20 Turns once hostilities commence.
It is characterized by tactical nuclear weapons.
It is characterized by a few political rules to complicate the military action.
It is characterized by a large number of units.
IsA: It is a tactical wargame at the divisional level.
KnownAs: It is known as Group of Soviet Forces Germany in its 2003 republication.
It is known as Brezhnev's War: NATO vs. the Warsaw Pact in Germany, 1980 in its 2018 reimplementation.
KnownFor: It is known for its back-and-forth nature.
It is known for its thrilling gameplay.
It is known for its strategic depth.
It is known for its complexity.
It is known for its satisfying game experience.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
PublishedAs: It was published as Group of Soviet Forces Germany in Strategy & Tactics #220.
It was published as Brezhnev's War: NATO vs. the Warsaw Pact in Germany, 1980.
PublishedBy: It was published by Decision Games.
It was published by Compass Games.
PublishedIn: It was published in Strategy & Tactics #220.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating an invasion of Western Europe by the Warsaw Pact.
It is used for simulating a surprise attack by Warsaw Pact forces against NATO forces defending Western Europe in the 1970s.
It is used for simulating combat.
It is used for military action.
